svg.title: System Design: Problems & Solutions Flowchart
svg.desc: Complete flowchart showing all scaling problems and their solutions from single server to millions of users

text.style: font-size: 18px; font-weight: 600; fill: #fff; font-family: 'Anthropic Sans', sans-serif;
font-size: 15px; font-weight: 600; fill: #888; font-family: sans-serif; letter-spacing: 1px;

svg.text: Web + DB + Cache on one machine
Daily backups to S3. Manual recovery. RTO = hours

text.style: font-size: 15px; font-weight: 600; fill: #888; font-family: sans-serif; letter-spacing: 1px;

svg.text: STAGE 2: SEPARATE TIERS + DATABASE TYPE & SCALING CHOICE
Separate Web Tier from Data Tier
Web & DB fight for CPU, memory, disk
Each tier scales independently. No more resource conflict
Structured? Need ACID? Speed or consistency?

text.style: font-size: 15px; font-weight: 600; fill: #888; font-family: sans-serif; letter-spacing: 1px;

svg.text: Question: How do users find them? Which server to connect?
Traffic uneven, no failover if server goes down
Routes to private IPs (10.0.0.1-3). Health checks. Round-robin distribution.
Server down → automatic failover. Traffic evenly distributed.

text.style: font-size: 15px; font-weight: 600; fill: #888; font-family: sans-serif; letter-spacing: 1px;

svg.text: Master crashes → no reads/writes → app down
Master (10.0.0.100): ALL writes. Slaves (10.0.0.101-102): read copies
If master fails → promote slave. Replication lag <100ms. Eventual consistency.

text.style: font-size: 15px; font-weight: 600; fill: #888; font-family: sans-serif; letter-spacing: 1px;

svg.text: ❌ Problem 5.1: Database Hammered by Repeated Reads
3 servers × 100 users = 300 identical queries/sec → DB overload
Cache hit <1ms. Cache miss → DB, store in cache. Hit rate 90%+
Result: DB load drops 10×, latency 100× faster
Cache holds old data, user sees stale profile
Short TTL or proactive delete on write
Cache down → all misses → DB hammered
Cache at capacity. Which entries evict?

text.style: font-size: 15px; font-weight: 600; fill: #888; font-family: sans-serif; letter-spacing: 1px;

svg.text: User in London hits US server → 120ms vs 30ms from local edge
Serve static assets from edge near users. 30ms instead of 120ms
Bandwidth offload. Result: 4× faster, less origin load
CDN serves old assets for hours
Request from origin if CDN fails

text.style: font-size: 15px; font-weight: 600; fill: #888; font-family: sans-serif; letter-spacing: 1px;

svg.text: User A logs in on Server 1 → load balancer MUST route to 1 always
Can't add/remove servers without logging out users
✓ Solution 7.1: Move State to Persistent Store
Store sessions in Redis/DB (10.0.0.50), NOT server memory
Any server can fetch session. LB routes to ANY server. Auto-scaling works!

text.style: font-size: 15px; font-weight: 600; fill: #888; font-family: sans-serif; letter-spacing: 1px;

svg.text: STAGE 8: MULTI-DATA CENTER + STAGE 9: MESSAGE QUEUE + STAGE 10: SHARDING
Billions of rows, master can't handle

text.style: font-size: 15px; font-weight: 600; fill: #888; font-family: sans-serif; letter-spacing: 1px;

svg.text: ✓ SYSTEM NOW SCALES TO MILLIONS OF USERS
High availability · Low latency · Automated operations · Disaster recovery
